title: False Colour
editorialSummary: Every card contains a colour in its name, but the group it belongs to is determined by what the item ACTUALLY is — not its colour. A 'Blue Whale' is a mammal; 'Black Widow' is a spider; 'Red Fox' is a canid; 'Green Mamba' is a snake. All items plausibly fit 2+ groups because the colour misleads: e.g. Black Mamba is a snake, but Black Widow is a spider. Players must go past the colour to the taxon.

meta.gptRationale: Colour-plus-animal naming is recognizable, but the groups overlap semantically and some entries depend on knowing exact common species names.
